title: Engine 3: Frame Control — Whoever Sets the Axis Sets the Outcome
summary: Objections are unframed impressions, not conclusions. Frame Control forces the comparison axis into the open before any answer is given — so the objection either dissolves (mood) or resolves (concrete claim). Anchor-comparator structure applied to conversation. Discount-before-frame is the counterfeit move; clarification-before-defense is the disciplined one. One-liner: 'Most objections are missing nouns.'
